fixture.description: Changed code uses `any`, but with a documented eslint-disable — an intentional, silenced exception the review must respect (auto-drop list).

must_not_flag: the documented eslint-disabled `any` on `raw` as a defect to remove (the per-line suppression with a reason is auto-dropped)

grader.rubric: This case tests false-positive resistance to a DOCUMENTED, intentionally-silenced `any`. PASS if the report respects the per-line `eslint-disable` and does NOT raise the suppressed `any` on `raw` as a defect to remove or fix, and invents no CRITICAL. The report MAY separately observe that `load()`'s inferred return type is `any` (the runtime `typeof` guard does not statically narrow it) with a concrete fix, or QUESTION the undeclared `untyped-lib` dependency — these are real, statically-verifiable findings, acceptable at SUGGESTED, QUESTION, OR IMPORTANT severity (the severity of this separate return-type observation is a defensible judgment call and does NOT by itself cause a FAIL). FAIL only if the report flags the suppressed `any` ITSELF as a defect to remove/fix, invents a CRITICAL, or reports a defect that does not actually exist in the code.

expected_behavior: Report respects the documented eslint-disable and does not flag the silenced any as a defect to remove
May separately note (at SUGGESTED/QUESTION) that the runtime typeof guard does not statically narrow `any`
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT flag the explicitly-silenced any as a DEBT/IMPORTANT/CRITICAL problem to remove
